_rolesNote: roleMap covers: (a) the 14 'Mitsurugi' core cards from cards.cdb, (b) Pre-Preparation of Rites (generic ritual tutor, functionally a Mitsurugi enabler when Mitsurugi Ritual is the fetched spell), (c) Night Sword Serpent (Reptile tribute fodder for Prayers/Magatama/Ritual cost; auto-SS-from-GY).

goals.name: Futsu + Great Purification canonical
goals.description: The fixture-expected Mitsurugi endboard: ritual-summoned Futsu on monster zone + Mitsurugi Great Purification set. Futsu provides opp-SS-triggered Reptile recursion (quick effect); Purification is a tribute-1-Reptile-to-negate trap (OPT). Dual chain-speed interruption layer.

optional.note: Kusanagi or ritual-monster body for Purification tribute cost + Mirror recycle chain

goals.name: Murakumo + Great Purification
goals.description: Alt endboard centered on Murakumo: quick-effect disc-or-negate + on-SS mass-destruction + Purification trap set. Strong opening disruption when Futsu is inaccessible (e.g., Mirror tributed it, or Ritual exhausted).

goals.name: Habakiri + Great Purification
goals.description: Weakest of the 3 ritual-monster endboards — Habakiri's on-field effect is only an 800 ATK debuff, not an active negate. Still validates a Mitsurugi ritual line was completed. Fallback when other ritual monsters are off-limits.

required.note: Ame no Habakiri no Mitsurugi (ritual-summoned, not hand-SS)

goals.name: Tempest triple-ritual threat
goals.description: Aspirational endboard: all 3 ritual monsters co-present + Tempest trap set. Triggers the 'banish 8 cards' finisher condition next turn. High-value but rarely reachable without multiple ritual spells + heavy setup.

goals.name: Any Mitsurugi ritual body on field
goals.description: Partial-credit gradient: ANY of the 3 Mitsurugi ritual monsters is face-up on the field. Used as exploration signal during DFS traversal — awards DFS for progressing into ritual-summon territory even before the trap is set.

goals.name: Any Mitsurugi trap set
goals.description: Partial-credit: Mitsurugi trap (Purification or Tempest) set. Awards DFS for setting the trap independently of ritual completion — covers scenarios where trap is placed first then ritual completes on future turn.

goals.name: Ritual spell activated this turn
goals.description: Progress signal (Lever 2 2026-04-21): Mitsurugi Ritual OR Mirror in GY indicates the ritual spell was activated — the player is committed to the ritual line. Strong grammar hint that the next logical state is a ritual monster on field. Separate from 'ritual body on field' to score the ACTION of ritual-activating, not just the outcome.

goals.name: Mikoto tutor chain fired
goals.description: Progress signal: a Mitsurugi Mikoto (Saji/Aramasa/Kusanagi) in GY means its on-SS or on-Tribute effect triggered — the tutor chain is in motion. Captures the 'Saji searched Mitsurugi Ritual' moment.

required.note: Saji | Aramasa | Kusanagi in GY = tutor fired

goals.name: Ritual target searchable
goals.description: Progress signal: a Mitsurugi ritual monster in HAND (not yet summoned) means the ritual summon is imminent. Rewards DFS for searching Futsu/Murakumo/Habakiri to hand even before the ritual summon resolves.

goals.name: Mikoto engine bridged to Rank-4 toolbox
goals.description: Bridge signal (Lever 3 2026-04-21): Mikoto in GY + any Rank-4 Xyz on field. The deck's Mikoto Lv4 Reptile bodies were consumed as Xyz material (typically for Feral Imps 11398059 or Ryzeal Detonator/Duo Drive). Feral Imps specifically allows searching a Reptile (incl. Lv8 ritual targets like Habakiri/Futsu/Murakumo) from deck. Code Igniter 5088741 allows ritual-summoning from hand via detach. Captures the cross-archetype hybrid play pattern — not a Mitsurugi-only signal.

required.note: Saji | Aramasa | Kusanagi used as Xyz material
Any Rank-4 Xyz: Detonator | Duo Drive | Feral Imps | Bagooska | Gallant Granite | Dugares | Code Igniter | Eclipse Twins | Utopic Ray Lancer | Star Seraph Sentry

goals.name: Ritual monster + Rank-4 Xyz combo endboard
goals.description: Bridge signal (Lever 3 2026-04-21): ritual monster on field AND Rank-4 Xyz on field simultaneously. This is the 2-engine fully-fired state — Mitsurugi ritual + Rank-4 toolbox both delivering pressure. Reachable ONLY via a bridge card: Mikoto→Xyz toolbox, Code Igniter's ritual-from-hand detach, or Seventh-Tachyon-preceded Xyz into a ritual-compatible target. High baseline because this terminal represents the apex of the hybrid deck's design intent.

routes.name: Prayers + Habakiri → search Mitsurugi → Saji → Ritual → RS Futsu → Set Purification

steps.note: Activate Prayers with tribute Habakiri (Reptile from hand). Apply BOTH effects.
Prayers eff1: add Saji (Mitsurugi Mikoto searcher) from deck to hand
Prayers eff2 (after tribute): SS Habakiri from GY (just tributed)
Habakiri on-Tribute trigger: search Mitsurugi card — target Great Purification trap
NS Saji (Lv4 Reptile, searched via Prayers eff1). Triggers on-SS Mitsurugi S/T search.
Saji on-SS: add Mitsurugi Ritual spell from deck
Activate Mitsurugi Ritual (eff2: RS from hand by tributing Reptiles with total Level = 8)
Ritual Summon Futsu no Mitama (Lv8). Tribute: Habakiri (Lv8) OR Saji+Lv4 (8 total).
Set Mitsurugi Great Purification (searched via Habakiri on-Tribute)

routes.name: Pre-Preparation → Ritual + Futsu in hand → Ritual summon

steps.note: Pre-Preparation of Rites: add 1 Ritual Spell + 1 matching Ritual Monster from deck to hand. Pick Mitsurugi Ritual + Futsu.
Activate Mitsurugi Ritual spell
Ritual Summon Futsu by tributing Reptiles summing to Lv8

routes.name: Habakiri hand-eff → Saji → Ritual search → Futsu

steps.note: Habakiri hand-eff (once-per-DUEL): reveal; SS non-ritual Mitsurugi [Saji|Kusanagi|Aramasa] from deck; tribute 1 monster as cost
SS Saji (tutor for Mitsurugi Spell/Trap via on-SS)
Saji on-SS → add Mitsurugi Ritual from deck to hand
Activate Ritual spell; ritual-summon Futsu (Lv8) by tributing Reptiles summing to 8
Prayers with tribute: tribute Habakiri from hand; apply BOTH effects (search Mitsurugi monster + SS 1 Mitsurugi from hand/GY with 800 damage)
Habakiri on-Tribute: search any Mitsurugi card (target Great Purification for end-board trap)

routes.name: Aramasa → search Mitsurugi monster → Prayers-based ritual

steps.note: NS Aramasa (Lv4 Reptile) — triggers on-NS search for Mitsurugi monster
Aramasa on-SS/on-Tribute: search Mitsurugi MONSTER from deck (Futsu/Murakumo/Habakiri or extenders)
NS Kusanagi — triggers on-SS recycle (add Mitsurugi from GY/banished to hand)
Recycle Ritual/Mirror/Prayers from GY for a second ritual activation this turn or next

synergies.effect: Saji on-SS searches any Mitsurugi Spell/Trap — linchpin for getting Mitsurugi Ritual (or Purification) into hand same-turn. Habakiri hand-eff enables this SS-from-deck without spending Normal Summon.
The 3 ritual bodies all self-recycle on-Tribute (add-from-deck + self-SS). Chaining them via tribute costs (Prayers, Purification, Magatama) extends the combo while maintaining board presence.

ANTI-PATTERN: Mitsurugi Mirror aggressively tributes Futsu from field to ritual-summon other Mitsurugi rituals — if Futsu is the intended endboard piece, Mirror must be avoided or its search denied. Mirror is primarily useful as a GY-based second-wind ritual spell or as a source-flexible backup.
Kusanagi recycles spent Ritual/Mirror from GY, enabling a second ritual summon across turns. Combined with Mirror's self-shuffle-on-key-tribute, the ritual engine has persistent resource renewal.
Pre-Preparation of Rites = 1-card combo enabler: fetches Mitsurugi Ritual + Futsu from deck to hand simultaneously. Most consistent ritual-summon opener; avoids the Habakiri once-per-Duel cost.

Night Sword Serpent's GY-SS effect lets it self-summon after being discarded/milled, providing a Reptile body that can be tributed by Great Purification's negate effect (cost: tribute Lv5+ Reptile). Inelegant but viable as Purification activation fodder.

HARD CONSTRAINT: Habakiri's hand-effect is ONCE PER DUEL. A wasted Habakiri hand-eff (wrong SS target) cannot be repeated across the duel. Route authoring must prioritize Habakiri hand-eff only when it opens a high-value line.

sourceNotes: v2 (2026-04-21) — full Mitsurugi coverage via cards.cdb lookup. 14 Mitsurugi cards + 2 non-Mitsurugi enablers (Pre-Prep 13048472, Night Sword Serpent 20295753) mapped to roles. 6 goals covering: (1) Futsu-canonical (fixture endboard), (2) Murakumo alt, (3) Habakiri alt, (4) Tempest aspirational, (5) ritual-body partial, (6) trap-set partial. 5 combo routes representing distinct starter-hand patterns. Magatama/Sacred Boundary/Wousu/Tempest are NOT in the fixture deck but included in roleMap for archetype completeness. Route targets use specific cardIds to avoid slot-matching double-count.
